examples.sentence: The village is only five miles away as the crow flies, but the winding road makes the journey much longer.
examples.translation: The village is only five miles away in a straight line, but the road makes it longer.

examples.sentence: If you look at the map, the two cities are about 20 kilometers apart as the crow flies, ignoring all the mountains in between.
examples.translation: On the map, the cities are 20 km apart in a straight line, without considering obstacles.

examples.sentence: As the crow flies, the lake is just beyond the forest, but to reach it you'll have to follow the trail around the hills.
examples.translation: In a straight line, the lake lies just past the forest, but you must take the trail around the hills.

examples.sentence: We measured the distance as the crow flies, which gave a shorter estimate than driving distance.
examples.translation: We measured the distance in a straight line, which was shorter than the distance by road.

examples.sentence: The guide said the campsite was 10 miles away as the crow flies, but the actual hike would be longer.
examples.translation: The guide said the campsite was 10 miles in a straight line, but the hike is longer.

examples.sentence: You might think the cities are close, but as the crow flies, they're actually quite far apart.
examples.translation: You may think the cities are near, but the direct distance between them is quite large.

examples.sentence: When calculating delivery times, companies often consider the distance as the crow flies to estimate fuel costs.
examples.translation: Companies use the straight-line distance to estimate delivery fuel costs.

examples.sentence: Sarah was devoted to her work as the day is long, never leaving a task unfinished.
examples.translation: Sarah was completely devoted to her work, never leaving any task unfinished.

examples.sentence: He loves his family as the day is long, always putting their needs before his own.
examples.translation: He loves his family fully, always prioritizing their needs.

examples.sentence: As the day is long, the children played outside until the sun set and darkness fell.
examples.translation: The children played outside to the fullest extent until night.

examples.sentence: I trust him as the day is long; there’s no doubt about his honesty.
examples.translation: I completely trust him; there is no doubt about his honesty.

examples.sentence: She stood by her friends as the day is long, never wavering through thick and thin.
examples.translation: She supported her friends steadfastly, without wavering.

examples.sentence: The old oak tree has stood firm as the day is long, enduring every season and storm.
examples.translation: The old oak tree has endured steadfastly through every season and storm.

examples.sentence: They worked tirelessly as the day is long to complete the project on time.
examples.translation: They worked consistently and without stopping to finish the project.

examples.sentence: From the hilltop, you can see the cabin as the gull flies, right across the bay.
examples.translation: From the hilltop, you can see the cabin in a straight line directly across the bay.

examples.sentence: The island lies just 3 miles away as the gull flies, though the boat ride takes much longer.
examples.translation: The island is 3 miles away in a straight line, but the boat trip is longer.

examples.sentence: As the gull flies, the distance between the two points is shorter than the winding path through the forest.
examples.translation: In a straight line, the two points are closer than the winding forest path.

examples.sentence: We estimated the distance as the gull flies before setting up the GPS for the trek.
examples.translation: We measured the straight-line distance before using the GPS for the hike.

examples.sentence: The poet described the journey as being only a few leagues as the gull flies, emphasizing the directness of the path.
examples.translation: The poet said the journey was a few leagues in a straight line, emphasizing its directness.

examples.sentence: When asked about the distance, he said it was about 15 kilometers as the gull flies, not accounting for the hills.
examples.translation: He said the distance was 15 km in a straight line, ignoring the hills.

examples.sentence: As the gull flies, the town is closer than it seems when following the road along the coast.
examples.translation: The town is closer in a straight line than it appears by the coastal road.

examples.sentence: Jessica is as nervous about the interview as the next girl; it’s a big opportunity for all of us.
examples.translation: Jessica is just as nervous as any other girl about the interview.

examples.sentence: I don’t think she’s any more talented than the next girl in the chorus; they all have their strengths.
examples.translation: I believe she is no more talented than any other girl in the chorus.

examples.sentence: When it comes to wanting a fresh start, she’s as hopeful as the next girl.
examples.translation: She is just as hopeful as any other girl for a fresh start.

examples.sentence: I worry about fitting in at the new school as the next girl does; it’s a natural feeling.
examples.translation: I worry about fitting in just like any other girl; it’s natural.

examples.sentence: She hopes for a happy relationship as the next girl, sharing the same dreams and fears.
examples.translation: She shares the same hopes and fears for a happy relationship as any other girl.

examples.sentence: When it comes to feeling overwhelmed, she’s as vulnerable as the next girl.
examples.translation: She is just as vulnerable as any other girl when feeling overwhelmed.

examples.sentence: I want to succeed in this project as the next guy, putting in all the effort I can.
examples.translation: I want to succeed just as much as any other person, working hard.

examples.sentence: He enjoys a cold drink after work as the next guy does, especially on hot summer days.
examples.translation: He enjoys a cold drink after work just like anyone else.

examples.sentence: You can’t expect me to tolerate that nonsense any more than as the next guy would.
examples.translation: I won’t tolerate that nonsense any more than anyone else.

examples.sentence: I’d join the party as the next guy, but I’ve got an early meeting tomorrow.
examples.translation: I would go to the party just as willingly as anyone else, but I have work early.

examples.sentence: He accepted the challenge as the next guy would, without hesitation or fear.
examples.translation: He accepted the challenge as readily as anyone else.

examples.sentence: If you want to try the new restaurant, I’m as willing as the next guy to give it a shot.
examples.translation: I am just as willing as anyone else to try the new restaurant.

examples.sentence: When it comes to loving a good movie, I’m as much a fan as the next guy.
examples.translation: I share the same love for good movies as most people.

examples.sentence: She enjoys a weekend getaway as the next guy, appreciating the break from routine.
examples.translation: She enjoys weekend trips just like most people.

examples.sentence: I feel frustrated by the traffic jam as the next guy does; it’s part of city life.
examples.translation: I feel the same frustration about traffic as most people.

examples.sentence: I worry about job security as the next person, especially in uncertain times.
examples.translation: I worry about job security just like any other person.

examples.sentence: She wants to be heard as the next person does; everyone deserves a voice.
examples.translation: She wants to be heard just like any other person.

examples.sentence: We all hope for peace as the next person, regardless of background.
examples.translation: We all hope for peace equally.

examples.sentence: I’m no more likely to take risks than the next person in this office.
examples.translation: I am not more likely to take risks than anyone else here.

examples.sentence: She’s no less capable than the next person when it comes to handling pressure.
examples.translation: She is equally capable as others under pressure.

examples.sentence: You’re no more disposed to change your mind than the next person, it seems.
examples.translation: You are not more likely to change your opinion than anyone else.

examples.sentence: I enjoy a good book as the next person, nothing unusual about that.
examples.translation: I like reading just as much as anyone else; it is not exceptional.

examples.sentence: She’s a bit shy, but then, as the next person, she has her own quirks.
examples.translation: She is shy, but like most people, she has her own traits.

examples.sentence: He prefers coffee in the morning, as the next person might, nothing surprising.
examples.translation: He likes coffee in the morning just like most people.

examples.sentence: The old flag flew proudly as the wind blows throughout the entire summer festival.
examples.translation: The flag flew continuously for as long as the wind blew during the festival.

examples.sentence: As the wind blows, the sails of the ship kept the boat moving steadily across the sea.
examples.translation: While the wind blew, the sails kept the boat moving steadily.

examples.sentence: We stayed outside as the wind blows, enjoying the fresh air until it finally died down.
examples.translation: We remained outside for as long as the wind continued.

examples.sentence: The leaves danced around the garden as the wind blows, never stopping until night fell.
examples.translation: The leaves moved continuously while the wind blew until night.

examples.sentence: She changes her mind as the wind blows, never sticking to one opinion for long.
examples.translation: She changes her opinions unpredictably, like the wind.

examples.sentence: His loyalties shift as the wind blows, making it hard to know where he stands.
examples.translation: His loyalties change freely and unpredictably.

examples.sentence: You can’t rely on that plan; it shifts as the wind blows and lacks consistency.
examples.translation: The plan changes unpredictably and is unreliable.

examples.sentence: The autumn leaves were carried away as the wind blows, drifting wherever the breeze took them.
examples.translation: The leaves were carried by the wind, moving wherever it blew.

examples.sentence: Rumors spread as the wind blows through the town, changing direction and intensity.
examples.translation: Rumors spread unpredictably, like the wind.

examples.sentence: Feelings can shift as the wind blows, sometimes sudden and unpredictable.
examples.translation: Emotions can change suddenly and unpredictably like the wind.

examples.sentence: That bargain is as cheap as they come; you won’t find a better deal anywhere.
examples.translation: That is one of the cheapest bargains possible; you cannot find cheaper.

examples.sentence: When it comes to loyalty, John is as faithful as they come.
examples.translation: John is extremely loyal, the most faithful you can find.

examples.sentence: This is as good a cup of coffee as they come, rich and freshly brewed.
examples.translation: This coffee is among the best possible.

examples.sentence: The guests arrived as they come, some early and others fashionably late.
examples.translation: The guests arrived in the order and timing they arrived, some early and some late.

examples.sentence: Orders will be processed as they come, so please be patient with the wait times.
examples.translation: Orders will be handled in the order they are received.

examples.sentence: The packages were delivered as they come throughout the day, without a fixed schedule.
examples.translation: Packages arrived throughout the day in the order they came.

examples.sentence: We’ll deal with the problems as they come, one step at a time.
examples.translation: We will handle problems in the order they appear.

examples.sentence: You have to take the challenges as they come and not get overwhelmed by what-ifs.
examples.translation: You must handle challenges when they occur, not worry about hypothetical problems.

examples.sentence: She meets difficulties as they come, facing each one with calm determination.
examples.translation: She deals with each difficulty as it arises, calmly.

examples.sentence: Problems in this job are as they come; you never know what to expect next.
examples.translation: Problems arise unpredictably and must be dealt with as they occur.

examples.sentence: I’ll come to the meeting as well, to hear what everyone has to say.
examples.translation: I will also come to the meeting to listen.

examples.sentence: She enjoys hiking as well as swimming during her vacations.
examples.translation: She enjoys both hiking and swimming.

examples.sentence: Mark plays the guitar, and he sings as well.
examples.translation: Mark both plays guitar and sings.

examples.sentence: Since the store is closing soon, we might as well buy everything we need now.
examples.translation: Because the store is closing, it is reasonable to buy everything now.

examples.sentence: If you’re going to be late, you might as well call ahead and let them know.
examples.translation: If you will be late, it is sensible to call and inform them.

examples.sentence: It’s raining heavily, so we might as well stay indoors today.
examples.translation: Because of the rain, it is best to stay inside.

examples.sentence: It’s just as well that the meeting was postponed; I wasn’t ready.
examples.translation: It is fortunate that the meeting was postponed because I was unprepared.

examples.sentence: She missed the bus, but it was just as well; she caught up with friends at the next stop.
examples.translation: Missing the bus turned out to be fortunate because she met friends.

examples.sentence: The weather cleared up after we left; it was just as well we didn’t cancel our trip.
examples.translation: It was fortunate that we did not cancel because the weather improved.

examples.sentence: She can run as well as her brother, matching him step for step in every race.
examples.translation: She runs equally as fast as her brother.

examples.sentence: If you study as well as you work, you’ll do great in your exams.
examples.translation: If your studying is as good as your working, you will succeed.

examples.sentence: He plays chess as well as he plays checkers, showing skill in both games.
examples.translation: He is equally skilled in chess and checkers.

examples.sentence: They speak English as well as French, making communication easy in either language.
examples.translation: They are equally proficient in English and French.

examples.sentence: We have no confirmed plans as yet, but we’ll let you know when something is decided.
examples.translation: We do not have plans so far, but will inform you once decided.

examples.sentence: Has the package arrived as yet, or should I check with the courier?
examples.translation: Has the package arrived up to now, or should I inquire?

examples.sentence: As yet, no one has solved the mystery, but the investigation continues.
examples.translation: Up to now, the mystery remains unsolved.

examples.sentence: The results are not available as yet; please check back later.
examples.translation: The results are not ready until now; please check later.

examples.sentence: He hasn’t responded as yet, but I’m sure he will soon.
examples.translation: He has not replied so far, but likely will soon.

examples.sentence: As yet, the new policy has not been implemented across all departments.
examples.translation: Until now, the new policy is not implemented everywhere.
